name: inRiver Product Information Management API
what_it_does: inRiver product information management (PIM) REST API for manufacturers, brands, and retailers to manage product content, digital assets, and channel-specific data across omnichannel commerce. Enables AI agents to manage product entity creation and attribute management for product catalog automation, handle channel-specific product configuration and completeness scoring for omnichannel readiness automation, access digital asset link and media management for product content automation, retrieve product relationship and bundle management for product hierarchy automation, manage workflow and approval routing for product content governance automation, handle multi-language and locale-specific product content for global commerce automation, access product enrichment and data quality validation for catalog completeness automation, retrieve analytics and product readiness reporting for category management automation, manage channel syndication and outbound product data for marketplace distribution automation, and integrate inRiver with ERP systems, DAM platforms, e-commerce platforms, and marketplace syndication tools for end-to-end product information management.
use_cases: Enriching product data from catalog management automation agents
Preparing channel content from omnichannel readiness agents
Managing product relationships from catalog hierarchy agents
Syndicating product content from marketplace distribution agents
not_for: Real-time inventory management (use ERP or WMS APIs)
Customer pricing and quoting (use CPQ platforms)
Marketing content management (use Contentful or Sitecore)
best_when: A manufacturer, brand, or retailer wanting AI agents to automate product data enrichment, channel-specific content preparation, multi-language localization, and digital asset management within inRiver's PIM platform for omnichannel commerce.
avoid_when: PRODUCT CONTENT ACCURACY FOR REGULATED PRODUCTS: Automated product content enrichment via inRiver for regulated products (food, pharma, medical devices, electronics with safety warnings) must include required regulatory information; automated content generation without regulatory content validation creates product listing non-compliance for regulated product categories. DIGITAL ASSET COPYRIGHT AND LICENSING IN PIM: Automated digital asset ingestion and channel syndication via inRiver must verify asset licensing rights for each distribution channel; automated syndication of licensed images to unlicensed channels creates copyright infringement; implement asset license scope validation before automated channel syndication. PRODUCT CONTENT GDPR FOR CONSUMER PRODUCT REVIEWS: If inRiver PIM stores consumer-generated product review content, automated processing must comply with GDPR; personal data in product reviews requires consent management and retention controls.

auth.notes: inRiver uses API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Malmö, Sweden HQ (with North American office in Chicago). Founded 2007 by Johan Boström. Private (Accel, Summit Partners). Cloud-native PIM platform for manufacturers and brands. B2B and B2C commerce focus. Multilingual product content management. Channel readiness scoring. Native ERP and DAM integrations. Competes with Akeneo, Salsify, and Stibo Systems for enterprise PIM.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription based on product volume, user count, and channel count; implementation and professional services additional
pricing.notes: Malmö Sweden. Private (Accel, Summit Partners). Annual enterprise subscription. Product volume-based pricing. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; product and asset queries throttled; webhooks for product update and workflow events

known_agent_gotchas: API KEY FULL ACCOUNT ACCESS WITHOUT SCOPE GRANULARITY: inRiver API key provides full account access without fine-grained permission scope; automated product enrichment agents with API key can read and write all product data across all categories; implement application-level role restriction for multi-team automated workflows accessing specific product category subsets

PRODUCT ENTITY MODEL IS INSTANCE-SPECIFIC: inRiver product entity model (entity types, attributes, field groups) is customer-configured and unique per instance; automated integration code must query entity type and attribute metadata before product data operations; hardcoding attribute names from one implementation to another creates attribute mapping errors in automated product enrichment

CHANNEL READINESS SCORE DEPENDENCY ON COMPLETENESS RULES: inRiver channel readiness scoring is based on customer-configured channel-specific completeness rules; automated product readiness reporting must account for instance-specific readiness rule configuration; automated quality gate using readiness score without understanding configured completeness rules creates non-comparable quality thresholds

WORKFLOW APPROVAL STATE MACHINE FOR CONTENT GOVERNANCE: inRiver workflow engine manages product content approval state; automated product enrichment must respect workflow state machine transitions; automated attribute updates on products in approved state without workflow transition creates version conflict in content governance workflow

MEDIA AND DAM LINK MANAGEMENT FOR DIGITAL ASSETS: inRiver stores media links to external DAM systems rather than asset binaries; automated product data migration must maintain correct DAM asset references; automated product copy that duplicates entity without updating DAM links creates product records pointing to inaccessible or incorrect media assets
agent_readiness.security_notes: PIM platform. SOC2, GDPR. API key. EU/US. Product catalog, digital asset links, and channel content data.

security_score_components.security_notes: PIM. SOC2, GDPR. API key. EU/US. Product catalog and channel content data.
